prerequisites: A Mailgun account with a configured sending domain.
The account's private API key, obtained from the Mailgun dashboard under Account Settings then API Keys (this maps to the required private_key field).
Knowledge of which region your domain was created in (US or EU), since this determines the API base URL and the optional domain_region field.

authMethods.name: API key (HTTP Basic Auth)
authMethods.summary: Mailgun authenticates over HTTP Basic Auth. Supply the username 'api' and your Mailgun private API key as the password (curl --user 'api:YOUR_API_KEY'). This connector collects that key in the required, secret private_key field.

setupSteps.title: Retrieve your private API key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Mailgun dashboard, open Account Settings, then API Keys, and reveal your Private API key with the eye icon. Copy it and keep it secure; once a key is lost you must generate a new one.

setupSteps.title: Provide the private key
setupSteps.body: Enter the copied Private API key into the connector's private_key field. The connector uses it as the password in HTTP Basic Auth (username 'api').

setupSteps.title: Select the correct domain region
setupSteps.body: Set the optional domain_region field to match where your Mailgun domain was provisioned. US domains use the base URL https://api.mailgun.net/ and EU domains use https://api.eu.mailgun.net/. Use the region matching your domain so requests reach the correct endpoint.

setupSteps.title: Set an optional start date
setupSteps.body: Optionally provide start_date to bound how far back the connector replicates data. Leave it empty to use the connector's default behavior.

sources.title: Mailgun API Overview (base URLs for US and EU regions)
